Banisteria ternstroemiifolia is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Malpighiaceae. This plant is a part of the Malpighia genus. The Malpighia genus is further categorized under the order Malpighiales, a part of the clade rosids in the flowering plant family. Within the clade rosids, Malpighiales is classified under the superorder Rosanae and clade core eudicots. Further classification of Banisteria ternstroemiifolia is under the clade angiosperms.

intro: Banisteria caapi (also known as Banisteria ternstroemiifolia) is a tropical vine native to the Amazon rainforest, belonging to the Rubiaceae family. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including its ability to support weight loss, improve mental clarity, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression.
